commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR splits compaction notifyUser notices from internal callbacks and marks command-handler terminal replies with source-suppression-delivery metadata, with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +52. Total +76 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: Partially yes: notifyUser suppression is source-reproducible from the callback guard, and the remaining room_event delivery gap is source-reproducible from the dispatch predicate. The literal Feishu/WebChat authorized /compact report still lacks live channel proof.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can address the dispatch-layer command-reply gap and add the missing regression coverage without changing config or product policy.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): notifyUser independent of internal callbacks; mark command-handler replies (refs #87107)
target.body:
Refs #87107

### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #87107 reports two symptoms on OpenClaw 2026.5.22:
  - **Bug 2 (notifyUser)** — `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser: true` notices are silently skipped whenever an internal `onCompactionStart`/`onCompactionEnd` callback is registered (e.g. by Control UI). This is **source-reproducible on current `main`**.
  - **Bug 1 (compact reply silently dropped on Feishu DM / WebChat)** — reporter's paraphrased logs show `delivered=true` but no `deliver: sending text chunks`. **ClawSweeper review on this issue explicitly flagged this half as "needs a focused fixture or live channel reproduction"**, and a real-component sweep on current source (below) confirms the literal authorized-`/compact`-on-DM path does NOT reach the suppress branch — the policy short-circuits to `automatic` for explicit text-slash commands.
- **Root Cause**:
  - **Bug 2**: `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` conflates three audiences in one `else if` chain — internal callbacks (Control UI), `hookMessages` (plugin user-channel text), and the default `notifyUser` notice. The chain skips the default notice whenever an internal callback exists, which is wrong: internal callbacks and the user-channel notice serve different audiences.
  - **Bug 1 (adjacent hygiene, not the literal report)**: command-handler terminal replies leaving `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts` are not marked `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression`. Other system-meta notices (model-fallback warning in `agent-runner.ts`, embedded-runner errors in `payloads.ts`) already call `markReplyPayloadForSourceSuppressionDelivery` because they are not assistant source content. When source delivery is suppressed (`messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"` configs) AND the command turn is **not** explicit (skill-tool blocked replies, error replies, unauthorized text-slash replies), the suppress branch at `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` silently drops these via `logVerbose` + `continue`. The literal "authorized `/compact` on Feishu DM" path the reporter described does NOT hit this branch (policy returns `automatic` via `isExplicitSourceReplyCommand` short-circuit in `src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.ts`); the live drop in their logs still needs a live repro to pin which path on their build was actually hit.
- **Fix**:
  - **Bug 2**: split the conflated `else if` chain in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` (four `start`/`end`/`!completed end` branches) so internal callbacks fire independently of the user-channel notice. `hookMessages` still suppress the default notice in the same phase to avoid double user-channel text.
  - **Bug 1 (hygiene)**: apply `markReplyPayloadForSourceSuppressionDelivery` at the inline-actions chokepoint in `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts` for the five terminal `{ kind: "reply" }` returns (skill-tool not-available, blocked, success, error, terminal command reply). Mirrors the marker pattern already used elsewhere in the same suppress contract.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` — split the `else if` chain so the internal callbacks no longer gate user-channel notices.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts` — mark the five terminal command-handler replies via a local helper.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` — rewrote the existing test that locked in the buggy `else if` behavior; new test asserts both callbacks and both user notices fire.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts` — new test that asserts the marker on terminal replies.
- **What did NOT change**:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` — the suppress branch logic itself is untouched; only the replies feeding into it gain the marker.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.ts` — policy resolver untouched.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply-payload.ts` — marker helper unchanged.
  - Strict `room_event` (group chat) delivery — marker is intentionally rejected there by design (`dispatch-from-config.ts`: `ctx.InboundEventKind !== "room_event"` precondition). This PR preserves that.

### Reproduction

- **Bug 2 (notifyUser)** — unit test in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: with `compaction.notifyUser: true` and both `onCompactionStart` + `onCompactionEnd` registered, current `main` delivers 0 user-channel notices; this PR delivers both, while still firing both internal callbacks once each.
- **Bug 1 hygiene** — unit test in `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts` asserts terminal command replies carry `__openclaw.deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression: true`. Real-component tsx harness (verbatim output below) imports production `resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy` + `markReplyPayloadForSourceSuppressionDelivery` and replicates the dispatch decision in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` verbatim across four scenarios.

**Behavior addressed (Bug 2)**: split the `else if` chain so `compaction.notifyUser` notices are independent of internal Control-UI callbacks.

**Behavior addressed (Bug 1 hygiene)**: mark command-handler terminal replies so they bypass source-suppression in non-room_event, non-deny contexts where the command turn is not explicit (skill-tool blocked, unauthorized text-slash, non-command-source command-handler outputs).

**Real environment tested (real-component tsx harness against production policy + marker)**:

```
================ #87107 real-component scenario sweep ================

--- Scenario: Feishu DM, authorized /compact (text-slash, authorized=true)
    sourceReplyDeliveryMode = automatic
    suppressDelivery        = false
    before fix              = delivered
    after  fix (marker)     = delivered
    (fix no-op: already delivered)

--- Scenario: Feishu group room_event, authorized /compact
    sourceReplyDeliveryMode = message_tool_only
    suppressDelivery        = true
    before fix              = silently-dropped
    after  fix (marker)     = silently-dropped
    (fix no-op: still dropped — strict room_event excludes marker by design)

--- Scenario: Feishu DM, messages.visibleReplies=message_tool, NON-command reply (e.g., skill-tool blocked)
    sourceReplyDeliveryMode = message_tool_only
    suppressDelivery        = true
    before fix              = silently-dropped
    after  fix (marker)     = delivered
    >>> fix changes behavior: dropped → delivered

--- Scenario: Feishu DM, messages.visibleReplies=message_tool, UNAUTHORIZED /compact text-slash
    sourceReplyDeliveryMode = message_tool_only
    suppressDelivery        = true
    before fix              = silently-dropped
    after  fix (marker)     = delivered
    >>> fix changes behavior: dropped → delivered

================ VERDICT ================
FIX MEANINGFUL — marker recovers delivery in:
  • Feishu DM, messages.visibleReplies=message_tool, NON-command reply (e.g., skill-tool blocked)
  • Feishu DM, messages.visibleReplies=message_tool, UNAUTHORIZED /compact text-slash
```

**Exact steps or command run after this patch (touched-surface unit tests + real-component sweep)**:

```
node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/qmd87107/repro.mts   # real-component policy + marker sweep
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix (164/164 touched-surface tests + real-component sweep)**:

- Touched-surface vitest — 164/164 passing across the two touched files (`get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts` + `agent-runner-execution.test.ts`).
- Real-component harness verbatim output above. Marker recovers delivery in the two non-explicit-command scenarios; no-op (correct) for authorized-`/compact`-on-DM and `room_event` (by design).
- `git diff --check` clean.

**Observed result after fix (notifyUser delivery + adjacent suppress branch)**:

- Bug 2: with `compaction.notifyUser: true` and both internal callbacks registered, both internal callbacks fire AND both user-channel notices fire (was: 0 notices).
- Bug 1 hygiene: under `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, non-explicit command-handler terminal replies (skill-tool blocked, unauthorized text-slash) move from `silently-dropped` to `delivered` per the harness verdict above. Authorized `/compact` on Feishu DM was already `delivered` and stays so. Strict `room_event` group chat stays excluded by design (this PR does not change that).

**What was not tested**:

- The literal Feishu DM / WebChat authorized-`/compact` silent-drop the reporter described. The real-component sweep above shows that path resolves to `sourceReplyDeliveryMode=automatic` on current source via the explicit-command short-circuit, so the suppress branch is not entered. A live Feishu/WebChat repro on 2026.5.22 is still needed to pin whichever path the reporter actually hit. This PR uses `Refs #87107` rather than `Fixes #87107` for that reason.
- Strict `room_event` group-chat `/compact` delivery (marker is rejected there by design; out of scope).
- End-to-end Feishu/WebChat live channel proof (touched-surface unit tests + real-component policy sweep instead).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: marker on command-handler replies could mask a real suppression elsewhere. **Mitigation**: marker is only consulted under `suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery && ctx.InboundEventKind !== "room_event" && !sendPolicyDenied`; `sendPolicy: deny` and `room_event` group chats still drop the reply. Same suppress contract as the existing model-fallback + embedded-runner markers.
- **Risk**: independent `notifyUser` notice could over-notify when a plugin also sends a `hookMessage` for the same phase. **Mitigation**: `hookMessages.length > 0` still suppresses the default notice (same user channel; one text per phase preserved).
- **Risk**: rewriting the existing `prefers onCompactionEnd callback over default notice when notifyUser is enabled` test could mask a legitimate constraint. **Mitigation**: the previous assertion contradicts the docs contract (`docs/gateway/config-agents.md` says `notifyUser: true` sends brief notices when compaction starts and completes). The clawsweeper review on #87107 calls this out as a docs-vs-behavior mismatch.

### Change Type

fix

### Scope

`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts`, two adjacent test files.

### Linked Issue

Refs #87107
target.author: openperf
